summary: The principal should report suspected child sexual abuse to Child Protective Services (CPS) because teachers are legally designated mandatory reporters under state regulations who must initiate reports through child welfare authorities. This ensures compliance with legal mandates while maintaining necessary confidentiality protections during investigations.
